title: pentaschistis filiformis
intro: Pentaschistis filiformis, also known as the red tussock grass, is a species of flowering plant in the grass family, Poaceae. It is native to New Zealand and has been used by the indigenous Māori people for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in traditional Māori medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
